perQueryEvaluations.query: Find trading opportunities on KXMVESPORTSMULTIGAMEEXTENDED-S2026D6AEEE0583A-71524DD7E33: rank each sub-leg by expected-value given current yes_ask vs a naive prior, and flag any that look structurally cheap.

yes De'Aaron Fox: 2+... | Yes Price 50 | Naive Prior (Adj. Prob) 5.56% | Structurally Cheap? No
yes Jrue Holiday: 2+... | Yes Price 100 | Naive Prior (Adj. Prob) 11.11% | Structurally Cheap? No

scoreExplanations.specificity: Each sub-leg with exact yes_price and computed naive prior.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Overround + EV-vs-prior calculation is genuine contributor analysis.

scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Red flag: every row shows Naive Prior exactly 5.56% or 11.11% (1/18, 1/9) — uniform fractions suggest the 'naive' prior is just 1/N where N is outcome count, which produces misleading rankings when some legs have yes_price=50 and others 100 but all share denominators.

traceAssessment.executionIssue: Response body is truncated mid-table; synthesis or output size limit cut the final row.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: Truncated answer should have been flagged.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: Final visible row ends 'yes San Antonio wins by over 5.5 points | 100 |' — table cuts off before the naive-prior/flag columns.
traceAssessment.dataAccuracyRedFlags: Uniform 5.56%/11.11% naive priors look like 1/18 and 1/9; flagging any leg as 'structurally cheap' under this prior is fragile.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: responseText visibly truncates mid-row, suggesting synthesis output length cap or streaming truncation.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Strong analytical framing undermined by truncation and suspiciously uniform priors.
